topics: Hrs. 30 Min. Prerequisites (if any) PCCPT402 Course Type Practical Course Objectives

1. To create discrete time signals and test the sampling theorem. 2. To Formulate the DFT for a discrete signal and verification of its properties using Python/ Scilab/MATLAB. 3. To design and implement algorithms for solving difference equations

computing convolution and correlation and verifying their properties using numerical methods and simulations. 4. To design and test the filtering operations and compare with the theoretical values. 5. To implement the DSP computations on DSP hardware and verify the result Details of Experiment (Perform at least 10 Experiments from the list below
in which a minimum of 3 experiments with DSP kits are included.) Expt. No Experiment 1 Simulation of Signals

Simulate the following signals using Python/ Scilab/MATLAB. 1. Unit impulse signal 2. Unit pulse signal 3. Unit ramp signal 4. Bipolar pulse 5. Triangular signal 2 Verification of sampling theorem (use interpolation function) using Python/Scilab/MATLAB. 3 Verify the properties of DFT (like Linearity and Parseval 's theorem

etc. ) using Python/ Scilab/MATLAB. 4 Compute the DFT of square pulse and Sinc function using Python/ Scilab/MATLAB. 5 Find the DFT / IDFT of a given DT signal using Python/ Scilab/MATLAB. 6 Find Linear convolution using DFT(Overlap-add and Overlap-Save methods) in Python/ Scilab/ MATLAB. 7 Find Auto and cross correlation of two sequences and verification of their properties using Python/ Scilab/MATLAB. 8 Compute the N point DFT of a given sequence and plot magnitude and phase spectrum (using DFT equation and verify it by built-in routine) using Python/ Scilab/MATLAB. 9 Design and implement a Lowpass and High pass FIR filter to meet the desired specifications (using different window techniques) and test the filter with an audio file. Plot the spectrum of audio signal before and after filtering using Python/ Scilab/MATLAB. 10 Design and implement a digital IIR filter (Low pass and High pass) to meet given specifications and test with an audio file. Plot the spectrum of audio signal before and after filtering using Python/ Scilab/MATLAB. 11 Obtain the Linear convolution of two sequences using DSP kit 12 Obtain the Circular convolution of two sequences using DSP kit 13 Compute the N-point DFT of a given sequence using DSP kit 14 Determine the Impulse response of first order and second order system using DSP kit 15 Generate a sine wave and standard test signals using DSP kit 16 Sample an analog signal and study the aliasing effect using DSP kit Course Assessment Method (CIE

Electrical Engineering - NOC:Digital Signal Processing and its Applications Continuous Assessment (25 Marks) 1. Preparation and Pre-Lab Work (7 Marks) ● Pre-Lab Assignments
Assessment of pre-lab assignments or quizzes that test understanding of the upcoming experiment. ● Understanding of Theory
Evaluation based on students’ preparation and understanding of the theoretical background related to the experiments. 2. Conduct of Experiments (7 Marks) ● Procedure and Execution
Adherence to correct procedures
accurate execution of experiments
and following safety protocols. ● Skill Proficiency
Proficiency in handling equipment
accuracy in observations
and troubleshooting skills during the experiments. ● Teamwork
Collaboration and participation in group experiments. 3. Lab Reports and Record Keeping (6 Marks) ● Quality of Reports
Clarity
completeness and accuracy of lab reports. Proper documentation of experiments
data analysis and conclusions. ● Timely Submission
Adhering to deadlines for submitting lab reports/rough record and maintaining a well-organized fair record. 4. Viva Voce (5 Marks) ● Oral Examination
Ability to explain the experiment
results and underlying principles during a viva voce session. Final Marks Averaging
The final marks for preparation
conduct of experiments
viva

and record are the average of all the specified experiments in the syllabus. Evaluation Pattern for End Semester Examination (50 Marks) 1. Procedure/Preliminary Work/Design/Algorithm (10 Marks) ● Procedure Understanding and Description
Clarity in explaining the procedure and understanding each step involved. ● Preliminary Work and Planning
Thoroughness in planning and organizing materials/equipment. ● Algorithm Development

Correctness and efficiency of the algorithm related to the experiment. ● Creativity and logic in algorithm or experimental design. 2. Conduct of Experiment/Execution of Work/Programming (15 Marks) ● Setup and Execution
Proper setup and accurate execution of the experiment or programming task. 3. Result with Valid Inference/Quality of Output (10 Marks) ● Accuracy of Results
Precision and correctness of the obtained results. ● Analysis and Interpretation
Validity of inferences drawn from the experiment or quality of program output. 4. Viva Voce (10 Marks) ● Ability to explain the experiment
procedure results and answer related questions ● Proficiency in answering questions related to theoretical and practical aspects of the subject. 5. Record (5 Marks) ● Completeness
clarity
and accuracy of the lab record submitted SEMESTER S5 CYBER PHYSICAL SYSTEMS LAB Course Objectives

1. To identify Embedded components such as sensors and actuators 2. To design and test the calibration of sensors or controllers using microcontrollers like Arduino/Rasberry Pi or any compatible embedded platforms. 3. To model and develop simulations for CPS with real time data using MATLAB/SIMULINK or Gazebo tools. Details of Experiment (Perform at least 10 Experiments from the list below) Expt. No Experiment 1 Study about embedded components such as sensors and actuators. 2 Signal Integration
Differentiation
Logarithmic computation activities to understand the computational logic and the physical end result 3 Signal Addition

Subtraction and Multiplication computations activities to understand the computational logic and the physical end result 4 Implementation and calibration of humidity sensor with microcontrollers 5 Implementation and calibration of pressure sensor with microcontrollers 6 Implementation and calibration of temperature sensor with microcontrollers 7 Implementation and validation of RPM controller with microcontrollers 8 Implementation and validation of Illumination controller with microcontrollers 9 Implementation and validation of water level controller with microcontrollers 10 Implementation and validation of temperature controller with microcontrollers
